name: textual-border-box-height-auto
tags: textual tui border height auto zero-width zero-height layout bug css fixed height border-box widget invisible content task:e345785c
body:
In Textual (Python TUI framework), a widget with a fixed `height: N` (or `width: N`) combined with a `border`/`border-bottom` etc. will have the border eat into that fixed box, potentially squashing the actual content to zero size — with no exception thrown, it just silently renders blank/invisible.

Why: Textual uses a border-box model where border is drawn inside the declared box, not added outside it. `height: 1` + `border-bottom: solid` leaves 0 rows for content.

How to apply: prefer `height: auto` (or `width: auto`) whenever a widget has a border, margin, or padding and its content size isn't hardcoded elsewhere — auto-sizing correctly accounts for border+content. When a Textual layout looks broken/empty despite no errors in logs, headlessly inspect it via `app.run_test()` and check `widget.size` for each child (zero width/height is the signature of this bug) rather than assuming a logic error.
